steps.title: Identify the advantage the notes describe
steps.content: The notes contrast traditional platinum catalysts, which require large amounts of the rare and expensive metal, with Zhao's new catalyst, which is "highly effective" using only trace amounts. The key advantage is effectiveness achieved with far less platinum.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Researcher Ming Zhao and his colleagues created a platinum catalyst that combines platinum, a rare and expensive metal, with liquid gallium.: describes what the catalyst is made of but never mentions the efficiency advantage over traditional catalysts. ✗
  • Choice B — Like other platinum catalysts, the new platinum catalyst requires a particular amount of the metal to be effective.: wrongly implies the new catalyst requires a similar amount of platinum to traditional ones, contradicting the notes. ✗
  • Choice C — Platinum is a rare and expensive metal that is used as a catalyst for chemical reactions; however, platinum catalysts typically require a large amount of platinum to be effective.: restates the problem but says nothing about Zhao's solution or its advantage. ✗
  • Choice D — While still highly effective, the new platinum catalyst requires far less of the rare and expensive metal than do other platinum catalysts.: directly states both sides of the comparison — effectiveness maintained, platinum use dramatically reduced. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
